rationale: In Study 5 and the general discussion, the citation is used for a specific prior finding: manager political ideology is linked to firm risk behavior, often as a main effect. The authors then position their contribution as adding self-efficacy as a moderator and showing a contingent ideology-risk relationship at the firm level. So the cited paper is part of the argument structure that motivates hypotheses and interpretation, not just background name-checking.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Kashmiri and Mahajan (2017) as prior firm-level evidence on political ideology and corporate risk-related outcomes. It extends that line by introducing and testing self-efficacy as a moderator, arguing the ideology-risk association is conditional rather than purely a main effect.
